key_events.description: Technique executed on a GitHub Actions ephemeral Windows VM. Ephemeral runners are isolated per-job; no persistent outbound C2 or lateral movement observed across execution window.

sigma.title: Suspicious DNS Query with B64 Encoded String
DNS TXT Answer with Possible Execution Strings
Suspicious Cobalt Strike DNS Beaconing - DNS Client
DNS Query To Common Malware Hosting and Shortener Services
Suspicious Cobalt Strike DNS Beaconing - Sysmon

osquery.name: t1071_004_c2_sockets
osquery.query_text: SELECT p.pid, p.name, p.path, p.cmdline, s.remote_address, s.remote_port, s.state FROM process_open_sockets s JOIN processes p ON s.pid = p.pid WHERE s.remote_address != '' AND s.remote_address != '127.0.0.1' AND s.remote_address != '::1' AND s.state = 'ESTABLISHED' LIMIT 100;

osquery.name: t1071_004_dns_cache
osquery.query_text: SELECT pid, fd, socket, remote_address, remote_port FROM process_open_sockets WHERE remote_port = 53 LIMIT 50;

story.narrative: Execution, telemetry, detections, artifacts, network observations, and SOAR response are tracked as separate witnesses and tied together through shared anchors.

promotion.reason: Full S5 validation (Linux Docker sandbox): 3 direct run(s), 8/8 Sigma rules matched with event linkage, 0 osquery correlations confirmed, raw telemetry preserved, analyst reviewed, clean baseline.
